title: vigna parkeri subsp. parkeri
intro: Vigna parkeri subsp. parkeri, also known as the Indian bean or parker's bean, is a perennial climber belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Vigna parkeri subsp. parkeri include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of arthritis, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
